intro: On our ratings Vanta scores 3/5 for developer experience and 3/5 for transparency, against 4/5 and 3/5 for Drata. Both are in the security scanning space, but the day-to-day feel and how openly each vendor shares pricing and behavior differ. Beyond the headline, Vanta carries medium lock-in (medium — compliance evidence tied to to platform) and subscription from $10,000/year (Core); Drata carries medium lock-in (medium — compliance automation tied to) and subscription from $15,000-$25,000/year (Starter). Vanta is a fit when you are soc 2 type ii preparation and maintenance. Drata is a fit when you are mobile. The honest trade-off: Vanta is explicitly not for not targeting enterprise customers yet; Drata is explicitly not for hobby projects. On the product side, Vanta is described as SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and GDPR compliance automation with continuous monitoring and one-click auditor portal, and Drata is described as SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and GDPR compliance automation with continuous control monitoring and auditor portal.
quick_take: Vanta is for soc 2 type ii preparation and maintenance; Drata is for mobile; decide on developer-experience weighting.
verdict_a: Choose Vanta if you are soc 2 type ii preparation and maintenance and comfortable with its subscription from $10,000/year (Core).
verdict_b: Choose Drata if day-to-day developer experience matters more than the alternative (4/5 vs 3/5) and you are mobile.

faq.a: Vanta starts at $10,000/year (Core); Drata starts at $15,000-$25,000/year (Starter). On entry price alone that is the comparison, but real cost scales with users, events or volume, which the entry prices do not capture. Check each vendor's full tier list before committing.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Vanta to Drata?
faq.a: Migration from Vanta to Drata is feasible but not trivial: Vanta has medium lock-in (medium — compliance evidence tied to to platform). Core data can move; integrations and workflow logic need rebuild. Allow for a parallel-run period.

On our scoring Drata rates 4/5 for developer experience versus 3/5 for Vanta. That suggests Drata feels more polished for everyday developer tasks. We still recommend a short hands-on trial on your stack before standardising.

faq.q: Is Drata a good alternative to Vanta?
faq.a: Drata is pitched at mobile, which overlaps with Vanta's fit (soc 2 type ii preparation and maintenance) but is not identical. Note that Drata is explicitly not for hobby projects, so it only replaces Vanta if you are outside that exclusion. Feature-for-feature parity is not guaranteed, so confirm the specific capability you rely on before switching.
